data_policy.technical_note: Downloaded from a publicly queryable ArcGIS FeatureServer.
data_policy.reuse_note: Technical accessibility does not itself grant republication rights. Confirm CIR attribution and reuse terms before public redistribution.
data_policy.analytical_note: Rolling analytics use the latest event date in the dataset as the reference date.

analytics.reference_basis: All rolling periods are calculated relative to the latest event date in the CIR dataset, not the current date.

assessment.headline: CIR activity trend: decrease
assessment.summary: Verified incident activity decreased in the latest seven-day period. The dataset records 7 incidents, compared with 12 in the preceding seven days (-41.7%). Across the latest 30-day analytical window, ● Damage was the most frequently recorded category. Recorded activity was most concentrated around Gaza City during the same period. The 30-day window contains 39 incidents with a casualty-related signal and 9 incidents marked for ceasefire monitoring. The latest event in the source dataset is dated 2026-04-25. This assessment describes the CIR dataset and should not be interpreted as a complete or real-time record of all conflict activity.
paragraphs: Verified incident activity decreased in the latest seven-day period. The dataset records 7 incidents, compared with 12 in the preceding seven days (-41.7%).
Across the latest 30-day analytical window, ● Damage was the most frequently recorded category.
Recorded activity was most concentrated around Gaza City during the same period.
The 30-day window contains 39 incidents with a casualty-related signal and 9 incidents marked for ceasefire monitoring.
The latest event in the source dataset is dated 2026-04-25. This assessment describes the CIR dataset and should not be interpreted as a complete or real-time record of all conflict activity.
